title: allium sphaerocephalon subsp. durandoi
intro: Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. durandoi, also known as the round-headed garlic, is a subspecies of the allium sphaerocephalon plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the onion family (Amaryllidaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Allium sphaerocephalon subsp. durandoi include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and have antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into tinctures, infusions, or salves, and it is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
